text: In the coming decades, artificial intelligence is expected to evolve from narrow AI, which performs specific tasks, into general AI or AGI. AGI would be able to learn and reason like a human across a wide range of activities. This could bring major benefits, such as faster medical diagnoses, more efficient energy use, and solutions to complex global problems. However, there are also significant risks, including job displacement, loss of privacy, and the challenge of controlling superintelligent systems. Many experts predict that AGI could emerge between 2030 and 2050, though some believe it may take longer. Preparing for this future requires careful planning and international cooperation.
